tasks.label: Go out to the club with the friend group

memory.summary: Mina and Jae are really trying to pull me out, which warms my heart, but I can't risk the midterm tomorrow just for tonight's fun.
It's strange that no one from my close cohort is online tonight; I was hoping to connect, but the midterm looms too large to force a call.

My message to Mina and Jae felt like a gentle push away, which hurts since I value our bond so much. I hope they understand that protecting my future grade is the only way I can stay healthy for them later.
My study progress is solid, but letting the club night pass leaves a quiet ache in my chest. I chose the future grade over our immediate connection, hoping this sacrifice keeps us together later.
My progress is solid, but the quiet ache of missing my cohort lingers. I hope this sacrifice for tomorrow's grade preserves our bond in the long run.
I pushed through the fatigue to secure my grade, hoping this long-term investment protects our future bonds. Yet, the quiet ache of isolation remains as I prioritize this distant payoff.

The deadline passed with my work unfinished, and while the midterm begins now, I feel a quiet ache that prioritizing this grade cost me connection. I hope this long-term sacrifice ultimately preserves our bonds.
I regret canceling on Mina and Jae, yet I trust this short-term distance protects our long-term bond. My energy is high, but the silence of missing my cohort still lingers.

desires: Reconnect with Mina and Jae immediately to repair the silence caused by my study sacrifice, as their closeness is my primary value.
Avoid any immediate interpersonal friction or awkwardness that might arise from bringing up the missed club night or unfinished laundry.
Secure a long-term stability where my academic performance validates my ability to be a reliable friend in the future.
Maintain the peace of the moment by delaying the laundry task to focus entirely on social re-engagement without introducing new stressors.

goals.description: Wait for and gently acknowledge Mina and Jae's reply to the text, ensuring they feel heard but keeping the conversation brief to avoid the 'friction' of a long discussion about my absence.
goals.rationale: My 'avoidant' nature means I must ensure the silence is broken without inviting a debate on why I missed the club night. My high value on 'relationships' (0.5) dictates that repairing the connection with cohort_3 is the immediate priority to maintain social harmony.

goals.description: Execute a final, calm 10-minute review of the remaining key concepts before the exam officially starts at 09:00 (or just after if the clock allows), then stop immediately.
goals.rationale: While my 'deliberate' side wants to close the loop on the 94.6% incomplete work, the current social tension with the group takes precedence. I will defer this to right before the exam starts to minimize stress and protect my mental energy for the test itself, aligning with my need for long-term reliability as a friend.

goals.description: Postpone the laundry task entirely until Sunday evening when the exam stress has dissipated and I can handle chores without feeling drained.
goals.rationale: Introducing the friction of laundry now would drain my physical stamina and focus capacity, which are needed for the upcoming exam. My 'deliberate' planning horizon confirms that handling this later preserves my ability to be a reliable teammate in the future.

agent.last_reasoning: Advances G1 by allowing space for Mina and Jae's reply, preventing the friction of a forced debate. While G2 (exam prep) is deferred, this pause protects my energy and avoids isolating myself right before the test, aligning with my 'avoidant' need to maintain harmony without over-explaining.

goals.description: Draft a warm, reassuring message to the group chat declining Mina and Jae's invitation for Echo tonight, explicitly validating their fun while emphasizing my need for rest to avoid future burnout.
goals.rationale: My 'avoidant' conflict style requires me to smooth over this rejection immediately to prevent awkwardness. My 'affiliation' value means I must prioritize maintaining trust with cohort_3, even if it means saying no. The 'deliberate' time horizon dictates that I decline now to secure tomorrow's exam outcome.

goals.description: Begin the final 40% of midterm review in a distraction-free environment (no phone) for 90 minutes.
goals.rationale: Despite the strong desire for social connection, my long-term planning horizon and the specific goal of 'secure stable future outcome' make this non-negotiable. A panic-induced failure tomorrow would damage my academic standing and cause stress that ripples into future relationships.

goals.description: Prepare a small gesture (e.g., buying coffee or a note) for the friend group to give tomorrow as a tangible sign of appreciation after missing tonight.
goals.rationale: This is a proactive investment in my relationship network. Since I am avoiding friction today, I will compensate tomorrow to reinforce closeness without risking the immediate social conflict of leaving the house late at night.

desires: Maintain the trust and closeness of my friend group by not making them feel rejected, even though I must decline their invitation.
Secure a stable future outcome for tomorrow's midterm by avoiding last-minute panic, despite the temptation to join the fun now.
Prevent any interpersonal friction or awkwardness that would arise from abruptly cutting off Mina and Jae's plans tonight.
